message:
feat(engine): add closure/scope-chain infrastructure, destructuring lowering, and cross-cutting correctness fixes

Introduces the foundation for lexical scoping and closures in the baseline
interpreter and IR pipeline, alongside a sweep of numeric correctness and
safety improvements across the engine.

Closure and scope-chain support (bd-6a61n.1.1):
- Add ScopeChain, ScopeFrame, ScopeBinding, ClosureValue types in
  baseline_interpreter.rs for runtime lexical environment tracking
- Add 8 new Ir3 instructions: CreateClosure, PushCapture, PushScope,
  PopScope, DeclareBinding, LoadScoped, StoreScoped, InitBinding
- Wire instruction dispatch in the interpreter with full TDZ enforcement
  for let/const (UninitializedBinding error) and const-assignment
  protection (ConstAssignment error)
- Closure creation snapshots the scope chain; calls restore the captured
  environment so closures close over the defining scope
- Save/restore scope_chain depth across CallFrame boundaries
- Register new instructions in execution_orchestrator.rs opcode map

Destructuring lowering (bd-6a61n.1.5):
- Add lower_destructuring_to_ir1() in lowering_pipeline.rs to emit
  LoadBinding + GetProperty + StoreBinding sequences for object and
  array destructuring patterns
- Object patterns use property key strings; array patterns use numeric
  index strings; holes are correctly skipped
- Rest elements emit placeholder (runtime slicing not yet available)
- Nested patterns handled via recursion
- Fix variable declaration lowering to store to primary binding first,
  then destructure from it

Parser correctness:
- Fix numeric literal parsing (parse_i64_numeric_literal) to use u64
  intermediate parsing for hex/octal/binary/decimal, preventing overflow
  on values near i64::MAX/MIN boundary
- Fix trailing-comma array literal parsing to not leave spurious trailing
  None element
- Fix split_pattern_elements to use let-chain syntax consistently

Stdlib fixes:
- String.prototype.includes now respects the optional position argument
  using UTF-16 code-unit semantics
- Replace hand-rolled Newton's-method isqrt with stdlib .isqrt() across
  6 modules: stdlib.rs, catastrophe_witness_generator.rs,
  dp_budget_accountant.rs, regret_bounded_router.rs (eliminates
  convergence edge cases and clippy::manual_div_ceil warnings)
- Use saturating_add/saturating_mul in coordinate distance computation
- Fix percent_decode to validate UTF-8 before hex parsing

Safety hardening (unwrap_or(u32::MAX) -> expect()):
- Replace silent data corruption on u32 overflow with explicit panics
  in object_model.rs (alloc, alloc_proxy, alloc_callable, alloc_constructor),
  bytecode_vm.rs (NewObject, SetProperty slot resize, iterator alloc),
  closure_model.rs, deterministic_serde.rs, esm_loader.rs
- Use saturating_add for shape revision counter in bytecode_vm.rs
- Fix bytecode_vm SetProperty to resize slots vec when slot index exceeds
  current length (was silently pushing instead of indexing)

Entropy/evidence compressor robustness:
- Guard ArithmeticCoder against total_frequency==0 (division by zero)
- Use checked_add in SufficientStatistic::is_consistent to detect overflow
- Widen fisher_information computation to i128 to prevent overflow

Control-plane mock inventory improvements:
- Add SOURCE_DERIVED_SCANNER_VERSION and INVENTORY_FRESHNESS_POLICY_ID
  constants for inventory drift detection
- Add verify_inventory_freshness() for fail-closed re-scan comparison
- Fix brace-depth tracking in scan_file_for_seam_occurrences to process
  open/close braces character-by-character (was counting separately,
  causing scope-stack misalignment on lines with both { and })
- Switch build_canonical_inventory callers to use
  build_source_derived_inventory for workspace-aware scanning
- Refine orchestrator context refactor outcome logic for source-derived
  inventories

Resolver package index (bd-1lsy.5.8.1):
- Add new resolver_package_index.rs module with ART/MPHF-backed package,
  export, and subpath indexes for O(1) resolver queries

Other fixes:
- Replace unstable .is_multiple_of() with % 2 == 0 in
  wave_handoff_contract.rs and workload_corpus_gate.rs tests
- Include waiver hash, expiry, and status in zero_placeholder_gate
  input hash computation for deterministic gate evaluation

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1387,6 +1387,14 @@ impl ExecutionOrchestrator {
             crate::ir_contract::Ir3Instruction::EnterCatch { .. } => "enter_catch",
             crate::ir_contract::Ir3Instruction::EnterFinally => "enter_finally",
             crate::ir_contract::Ir3Instruction::EndFinally => "end_finally",
+            crate::ir_contract::Ir3Instruction::CreateClosure { .. } => "create_closure",
+            crate::ir_contract::Ir3Instruction::PushCapture { .. } => "push_capture",
+            crate::ir_contract::Ir3Instruction::PushScope => "push_scope",
+            crate::ir_contract::Ir3Instruction::PopScope => "pop_scope",
+            crate::ir_contract::Ir3Instruction::DeclareBinding { .. } => "declare_binding",
+            crate::ir_contract::Ir3Instruction::LoadScoped { .. } => "load_scoped",
+            crate::ir_contract::Ir3Instruction::StoreScoped { .. } => "store_scoped",
+            crate::ir_contract::Ir3Instruction::InitBinding { .. } => "init_binding",
         }
     }
